title: All Subsets With Duplicates Allowed
description:
Return all possible subsets of a list that may contain duplicates. Ensure the result does not contain duplicate subsets. Sort the array then skip equal elements at the same recursion depth.

    

Constraints:
    - 0 ≤ n ≤ 15
- Elements may contain duplicates
explanation:
Intuition
Sorting groups identical values. When iterating choices at a level, skip a value equal to the one just used to avoid regenerating the same subset structure.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Sort nums
2. Main Algorithm: For loop from index to n, skip if i>start and nums[i]==nums[i-1]
3. Return Result: Recurse after append, then pop

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(n·2^n)
- Space Complexity: O(n) recursion

Key Insights
- Sorting enables simple duplicate checks
- Skip works per depth, not globally

Common Pitfalls
- Skipping across recursion levels is incorrect
- Not sorting makes skipping unreliable
